old_exact: No comments or petitions were received to FILED JULY 15, 2020 CLERK OF THE SUPREME COURT STATE OF NORTH DAKOTArelocate or abolish this judgeship.
new_exact: No comments or petitions were received to relocate or abolish this judgeship.
source_quote:
No comments or petitions were received to

                                        1
 Relocate or abolish this judgeship. On July 9, 2021, the South Central Judicial
District filed a report containing population and caseload trends, and other
criteria identified in Section 4, N.D. Sup. Ct. Admin. R. 7.2.
evidence: Running header block 'FILED JULY 15, 2020 CLERK OF THE SUPREME COURT STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA' from page top was incorrectly merged into middle of paragraph 4, between 'received to' and 'relocate', breaking sentence flow and losing word space before 'relocate'. Source PDF shows legitimate page break with centered page number and form-feed between these phrases.

source_quote:
Hoffner testified he believes there is a three-
second delay between when one light turns red
evidence: Line break artifact: the PDF shows 'three-' at the end of a line, followed by a line break, then 'second' at the start of the next line. This is a compound adjective that should be joined as 'three-second' without the space introduced by line breaking.
note: The space after the hyphen is a byproduct of the newline character and should be removed to match the intended hyphenated form.

old_exact: Schuler did not make any for argument regarding the requirement "continuously [signaling] during not less than the last one hundred feet [30.48 meters] traveled by the vehicle before turning." in N.D.C.C. § 39-10-38(2)
new_exact: Schuler did not make any argument regarding the requirement in N.D.C.C. § 39-10-38(2) for "continuously [signaling] during not less than the last one hundred feet [30.48 meters] traveled by the vehicle before turning."
source_quote: [¶3] Schuler filed a motion to suppress challenging the legality of the stop, arguing a turn signal is not required prior to exiting a roundabout and the officer did not have reasonable suspicion to stop him. Schuler did not make any argument regarding the requirement in N.D.C.C. § 39-10-38(2) for "continuously [signaling] during not less than the last one hundred feet [30.48 meters] traveled by the vehicle before turning."
evidence: Database text has misplaced 'for' and reversed citation structure: 'for argument regarding the requirement "..." in N.D.C.C. § 39-10-38(2)'. Source PDF (PDF-authoritative) shows correct structure: 'argument regarding the requirement in N.D.C.C. § 39-10-38(2) for "..."'. This is a word-order/citation-placement error during ingestion.
note: The 'for' keyword is in wrong position; it should come after the statute citation, not before 'argument'. The quoted statute text should follow 'for', not precede the citation.

old_exact: requests for attorney fees under N.D.R.App.P. 38.
new_exact: requests for attorney's fees under N.D.R.App.P. 38.
source_quote: requests for attorney's fees under N.D.R.App.P. 38.
evidence: PDF source shows the possessive apostrophe (U+2019 curly quote) which should be converted to straight ASCII apostrophe (U+0027) per DB policy. The DB currently has no apostrophe at all, indicating OCR/ingestion loss of the possessive marker. The PDF clearly shows 'attorney's fees' (with apostrophe) while DB shows 'attorney fees' (no apostrophe).
note: This is not a straight-vs-curly quote policy violation. The DB correctly normalizes typographic quotes to ASCII, but in this case the apostrophe is completely missing. The straight ASCII apostrophe (U+0027) should be inserted to match the PDF's intended possessive form.

source_quote:
led an opinion concurring in
the result.

Austin Gunderson, Assistant State's Attorney, Mandan, ND, for plaintiff and
appellant.
evidence: The PDF (pdftotext -layout extraction) clearly shows 'Assistant State's Attorney' with the possessive form marked by U+2019 (RIGHT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK) followed by lowercase 's'. The database version has 'Assistant State' Attorney' with a straight apostrophe (U+0027) followed by a space, omitting the 's' entirely. This is an OCR/ingestion defect where the character immediately following the apostrophe was lost during processing. The corrected form uses the standard straight apostrophe per policy (Rule 9) while restoring the missing 's'.
note: The PDF uses curly quote (U+2019) + 's'; the DB should have straight quote + 's' per policy. The 's' is missing from the DB entirely, making this an OCR_CHAR error rather than merely a quote-style variance.

source_quote: See Interest of K.S.D., 2017 ND 289, ¶ 28, 904 N.W.2d 479; Shark v. Thompson, 373 N.W.2d 859, 869 (N.D. 1985).
evidence: The DB incorrectly has 'Shark introduced v. Thompson' but the source PDF has 'Shark' on line 206, followed by a page break with page number '4', then 'v. Thompson' on line 210. The word 'introduced' should not appear here at all. This is a citation that got corrupted during OCR/text extraction where a stray word was inserted.
note: The citation should read 'Shark v. Thompson' without 'introduced'. The page break artifacts in the source got collapsed and corrupted during ingestion.

evidence:
The DB text has an incomplete case citation missing the case name prefix 'State v.', the first page of the reporter ('253'), and the year and jurisdiction ('(N.D. 1977)'). The source PDF shows the complete citation. Verified by exact string search: 'Carlson, 258 N.W.2d at 258' occurs exactly once in DB (position 13151); 'State v. Carlson, 258 N.W.2d 253, 258
(N.D. 1977)' occurs exactly once in source (position 13910). After this fix, complete opinion body text matches source exactly (verified after whitespace and quote normalization).
note: This is a citation ingestion defect where truncation occurred. The full parenthetical with year and jurisdiction was dropped. Located in ¶18, the Fulks case discussion.

evidence: The DB text contains an incorrect pinpoint citation page number (424) for State v. Fields, 2003 ND 81, ¶ 11. The authoritative PDF source confirms the correct pinpoint is 242. This is a clear OCR digit substitution error (4→2 in the tens place). The citation appears exactly once in the DB and exactly once in the source with the correct value.
note: This is a HIGH-RISK citation error (pinpoint page number). The incorrect citation could mislead legal researchers. The correction is directly verified against the authoritative PDF source.

evidence: Citation verified by rendering page 4 of the PDF (mutool draw -r 300). The PDF clearly shows '2000 ND 3, § 16, 603 N.W.2d 906.' This is the citation to DeCoteau v.
